          Free Software Foundation
          59 Temple Place - Suite 330
          Boston, MA 02111-1307, USA

  As a special exception, if other files instantiate generics from this
  unit, or you link this unit with other files to produce an executable,
  this unit does not by itself cause the resulting executable to be
  covered by the GNU General Public License. This exception does not
  however invalidate any other reasons why the executable file might be
  covered by the GNU Public License.

  Tash is maintained at http://tcladashell.sourceforge.net/.

------------------------------------------------------------------

  This package was originally automatically generated from tcl.h at
  patchlevel 8.4.7.  Note that some of the comments below, preserved
  from tcl.h, do not apply to the Ada version.  Someday, these
  comments may be customized better.

------------------------------------------------------------------

simple_types.name: Tcl_EolTranslation
simple_types.code:
type Tcl_EolTranslation is
  (TCL_TRANSLATE_AUTO,
   TCL_TRANSLATE_CR,
   TCL_TRANSLATE_LF,
   TCL_TRANSLATE_CRLF);

simple_types.name: Tcl_ValueType
simple_types.code: type Tcl_ValueType is (TCL_INT, TCL_DOUBLE, TCL_EITHER, TCL_WIDE_INT);

array_types.name: Tcl_HashEntry_Array
array_types.code:
type Tcl_HashEntry_Array is
  array (CNatural range <>) of aliased Tcl_HashEntry;

array_types.name: Tcl_Obj_Array
array_types.code: type Tcl_Obj_Array is array (CNatural range <>) of aliased Tcl_Obj;

array_types.name: Tcl_RegExpIndices_Array
array_types.code:
type Tcl_RegExpIndices_Array is
  array (CNatural range <>) of aliased Tcl_RegExpIndices_Rec;

array_types.name: Tcl_Token_Array
array_types.code:
type Tcl_Token_Array is array
  (CNatural range <>) of aliased Tcl_Token_Rec;

record_types.name: Tcl_ChannelType_Rec

fields.name: typeName
fields.description: Version of the channel type.

fields.name: version
fields.description:
Procedure to call to close the channel, or TCL_CLOSE2PROC if
the close2Proc should be used instead.

fields.name: closeProc
fields.description: Procedure to call for input on channel.

fields.name: inputProc
fields.description: Procedure to call for output on channel.

fields.name: outputProc
fields.description: Procedure to call to seek on the channel. May be NULL.

fields.name: seekProc
fields.description: Set an option on a channel.

fields.name: setOptionProc
fields.description: Get an option from a channel.

fields.name: getOptionProc
fields.description: Set up the notifier to watch for events on this channel.

fields.name: watchProc
fields.description: Get an OS handle from the channel or NULL if not supported.

fields.name: getHandleProc
fields.description:
Procedure to call to close the channel if the device
supports closing the read & write sides independently.

fields.name: close2Proc
fields.description: Set blocking mode for the raw channel. May be NULL.

fields.name: blockModeProc
fields.description:
Only valid in TCL_CHANNEL_VERSION_2 channels or later:
Function to call to flush a channel. May be NULL.

fields.name: flushProc
fields.description:
Only valid in TCL_CHANNEL_VERSION_2 channels or later:
Function to call to handle a channel event.  This will be
passed up the stacked channel chain.

fields.name: handlerProc
fields.description:
Only valid in TCL_CHANNEL_VERSION_3 channels or later:
Function to call to seek on the channel which can handle
64-bit offsets. May be NULL, and must be NULL if seekProc is
NULL.

fields.name: wideSeekProc
fields.description:
Only valid in TCL_CHANNEL_VERSION_5 channels or later:
Function to call to truncate the underlying file to a
particular length. May be NULL if the channel does not
support truncation.

record_types.name: Tcl_CmdInfo_Rec

fields.name: isNativeObjectProc
fields.description:
1 if objProc was registered by a call to
Tcl_CreateObjCommand; 0 otherwise.
Tcl_SetCmdInfo does not modify this
field.

fields.name: objProc
fields.description: Command's object-based procedure.

fields.name: objclientdata
fields.description: ClientData for object proc.

fields.name: proc
fields.description: Command's string-based procedure.

fields.name: data
fields.description: ClientData for string proc.

fields.name: deleteProc
fields.description:
Procedure to call when command is
deleted.

fields.name: deletedata
fields.description:
Value to pass to deleteProc {usually
the same as clientData}.

fields.name: namespacePtr
fields.description:
Points to the namespace that contains
this command. Note that Tcl_SetCmdInfo
will not change a command's namespace;
use Tcl_RenameCommand to do that.

record_types.name: Tcl_EncodingType_Rec

fields.name: encodingName
fields.description:
The name of the encoding, e.g.  "euc-jp".
This name is the unique key for this
encoding type.

fields.name: toUtfProc
fields.description:
Procedure to convert from external
encoding into UTF-8.

fields.name: fromUtfProc
fields.description:
Procedure to convert from UTF-8 into
external encoding.

fields.name: freeProc
fields.description:
If non-NULL, procedure to call when this
encoding is deleted.

fields.name: data
fields.description:
Arbitrary value associated with encoding
type.  Passed to conversion procedures.

fields.name: nullSize
fields.description:
Number of zero bytes that signify
end-of-string in this encoding.  This
number is used to determine the source
string length when the srcLen argument is
negative.  Must be 1 or 2.

record_types.name: Tcl_Event_Rec

fields.name: proc
fields.description: Next in list of pending events, or NULL.

record_types.name: Tcl_Namespace_Rec

fields.name: name
fields.description:
The namespace's name within its parent namespace. This
contains no ::'s. The name of the global namespace is ""
although "::" is an synonym.

fields.name: fullName
fields.description: The namespace's fully qualified name.  This starts with ::.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Arbitrary value associated with this namespace.

fields.name: deleteProc
fields.description:
Procedure invoked when deleting the namespace to, e.g., free
clientData.

fields.name: parentPtr
fields.description:
Points to the namespace that contains this one. NULL if this
is the global namespace.

record_types.name: Tcl_NotifierProcs_Rec
Tcl_Obj_Rec

fields.name: internalRep
fields.description: @todo make precise rep for C union

record_types.name: Tcl_ObjType_Rec

fields.name: name
fields.description: Name of the type, e.g. "int".

fields.name: freeIntRepProc
fields.description:
Called to free any storage for the type's
internal rep. NULL if the internal rep
does not need freeing.

fields.name: dupIntRepProc
fields.description:
Called to create a new object as a copy
of an existing object.

fields.name: updateStringProc
fields.description:
Called to update the string rep from the
type's internal representation.

fields.name: setFromAnyProc
fields.description:
Called to convert the object's internal
rep to this type. Frees the internal rep
of the old type. Returns TCL_ERROR on
failure.

record_types.name: Tcl_Parse_Rec

fields.name: commentStart
fields.description:
Pointer to # that begins the first of one or more comments
preceding the command.

fields.name: commentSize
fields.description:
Number of bytes in comments {up through newline character
that terminates the last comment}.  If there were no
comments, this field is 0.

fields.name: commandStart
fields.description: First character in first word of command.

fields.name: commandSize
fields.description:
Number of bytes in command, including first character of
first word, up through the terminating newline, close
bracket, or semicolon.

fields.name: numWords
fields.description: Total number of words in command.  May be 0.

fields.name: tokenPtr
fields.description:
Pointer to first token representing the words of the
command.  Initially points to staticTokens, but may change
to point to malloc-ed space if command exceeds space in
staticTokens.

fields.name: numTokens
fields.description: Total number of tokens in command.

fields.name: tokensAvailable
fields.description:
Total number of tokens available at
*tokenPtr.

fields.name: errorType
fields.description:
One of the parsing error types defined above.

The fields below are intended only for the private use of
the parser.  They should not be used by procedures that
invoke Tcl_ParseCommand.

fields.name: strng
fields.description: The original command string passed to Tcl_ParseCommand.

fields.name: e_n_d
fields.description:
Points to the character just after the last one in the
command string.

fields.name: interp
fields.description: Interpreter to use for error reporting, or NULL.

fields.name: term
fields.description:
Points to character in string that terminated most recent
token.  Filled in by ParseTokens.  If an error occurs,
points to beginning of region where the error occurred
{e.g. the open brace if the close brace is missing}.

fields.name: incomplete
fields.description:
This field is set to 1 by Tcl_ParseCommand if the command
appears to be incomplete.  This information is used by
Tcl_CommandComplete.

fields.name: staticTokens
fields.description:
Initial space for tokens for command.  This space should be
large enough to accommodate most commands; dynamic space is
allocated for very large commands that don't fit here.

record_types.name: Tcl_RegExpIndices_Rec

fields.name: Start
fields.description: character offset of first character in match

fields.name: E_n_d
fields.description: character offset of first character after the match.

record_types.name: Tcl_RegExpInfo_Rec

fields.name: nsubs
fields.description:
number of subexpressions in the
compiled expression

fields.name: matches
fields.description:
array of nsubs match offset
pairs

fields.name: extendStart
fields.description:
The offset at which a subsequent
match might begin.

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved for later use.

record_types.name: Tcl_Time_Rec

fields.name: sec
fields.description: Seconds.

fields.name: usec
fields.description: Microseconds.

record_types.name: Tcl_Token_Rec

fields.name: typ
fields.description:
Type of token, such as TCL_TOKEN_WORD; see below for valid
types.

fields.name: start
fields.description: First character in token.

fields.name: size
fields.description: Number of bytes in token.

fields.name: numComponents
fields.description:
If this token is composed of other tokens, this field tells
how many of them there are {including components of
components, etc.}. The component tokens immediately follow
this one in the array of tokens of which this token is part.

record_types.name: Tcl_Value_Rec

fields.name: typ
fields.description:
Indicates intValue or doubleValue is valid, or both; or
wideValue.

fields.name: intValue
fields.description: Integer value.

fields.name: doubleValue
fields.description: Double-precision floating value.

fields.name: wideValue
fields.description: Wide (min. 64-bit) integer value.

documentation.description:
prepend \A to pattern so it only matches at the beginning of
the string.
Beginning of string does not match ^.
End of string does not match $.

generic_instantiations.name: Tcl_RegExpIndices_Pointers
generic_instantiations.code:
package Tcl_RegExpIndices_Pointers is new C.Pointers
  (Index => CNatural,
   Element => Tcl_RegExpIndices_Rec,
   Element_Array => Tcl_RegExpIndices_Array,
   Default_Terminator => (0, 0));

generic_instantiations.name: Tcl_Token_Pointers
generic_instantiations.code:
package Tcl_Token_Pointers is new C.Pointers
  (Index => CNatural,
   Element => Tcl_Token_Rec,
   Element_Array => Tcl_Token_Array,
   Default_Terminator => (0, C.Strings.Null_Ptr, 0, 0));

documentation.description:
Note, the Default_Terminator is required by
Interfaces.C.Pointers but isn't significant in a
Tcl_Token_Array, so don't use any of the subprograms that
depend on the terminator.
